signals.title: USD — Macro Inflation Surprise + Yield Curve
signals.text: USD at 98.77 following US Import Prices (+1.9% vs +1.0% est) and Export Prices (+3.3% vs +1.1% est) data. KC Fed Pres Schmid confirms inflation remains the 'most pressing risk,' supporting a higher-for-longer regime. US 2s10s spread at +44.5bp in a Bull Flattener regime indicates tightening cycle dominance. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp) and Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0% (+375bp) carry spreads remain the primary structural drivers as inflation data delays easing expectations.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.228 — AUD outperformance (avg +0.624%) vs JPY weakness (avg -0.539%) creates a 1.163% G8 spread. The move is fundamentally anchored by a +360bp OIS carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75%). Retail sentiment is at an extreme 85% short, diverging from COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long). This institutional-retail divergence suggests a contrarian risk of further upside as retail shorts are squeezed. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 17.9 (normal volatility).

signals.title: USD/JPY — Options Skew + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.123 — 25d Risk Reversal at -1.69 (Saxo) reveals a strong put skew, signaling the options market is paying a premium for downside protection despite the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage. USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation has broken to +0.503 (z=-2.24σ vs norm +0.146), indicating the pair is no longer tracking Treasury yields linearly. Hawkish BoJ signals are being offset by USD strength, but the options skew suggests institutional positioning for a potential intervention or policy shift.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Technical Break + Volatility Premium
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3726 testing a break above the 100-day MA (1.3718). Despite stronger Canada Wholesale Trade (+1.9% vs +1.4% est), the pair remains bid on USD momentum. Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% (+150bp) carry spread supports the upside. COT LF net short -46,889 (26.4% long) shows specs are heavily positioned against the CAD. Watch for a sustained close above 1.3720 to confirm the technical breakout.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Risk Premium
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7829 — IV at 12.3% vs HV30 at 7.4% generates a +4.9% Vol Risk Premium (VRP). This elevated premium indicates the options market is pricing in significantly higher volatility than has been realized over the past 30 days. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0% (+375bp) carry remains the widest in the G10 space, but the high VRP suggests market participants are aggressively hedging against a sharp CHF reversal or safe-haven bid.
